Pickleball growth map

Level up your pickleball game, choose smarter gear, and find people to play with.

Picklary brings together level guides, DUPR self-checks, gear criteria, pro-scene context, and review-first community tools in one pickleball hub.

Beginner Guide Pick my level Explore Gear Lab Find people to play with

Levels at a glance

  • 2.0New Player Foundation
  • 2.5Consistency Builder
  • 3.0Reliable Rally Player
  • 3.5Pattern and Pressure
  • 4.0Advanced Doubles Strategy
  • 4.5Tournament Pressure
  • 5.0Pro-Pattern Study

Beginner 2.0 → Advanced 5.0

DUPR pathway 2.0–5.0

2.5
3.5
Your first 30 days: a practice routine for 2.0–3.0 players Dinking fundamentals: staying steady at the kitchen line The third-shot drop, explained: why it matters and how to drill it Serve and return basics: common mistakes and fixes How to move from 2.5 to 3.0 in pickleball Pickleball 3.0 vs 3.5: what actually changes Who takes the middle ball in doubles pickleball? Third shot drop or drive: how to choose How to stop missing first in dink rallies Why backhand resets are hard and how to practice them How to choose a pickleball coach for your level

DUPR self-check →

◎

Why try it?

10 court scenarios reveal your shot-decision habits.

It is not an official rating, but it shows what you tend to choose under pressure and what to practice next.

10 scenarios3D courtPractice focus

Pick a level to see the next skill focus, gear clues, and practice path. Tap the numbers and icons on the rail to see the key points for each level.

Choose a DUPR level

2.0 2.5 3.0 3.5 4.0 4.5 5.0

Open any level from 2.0 through 5.0 directly.

Start here

Start, check your level, then choose gear

A simple path for first-time visitors: learn the basics, estimate your level, then compare paddles only after you know your needs.

Start here

Beginner Guide

Follow the rules, first shots, level path, and first-paddle checklist in order — built as the best entry point for new players.

Start the guide → Main tool

DUPR self-check

Answer ten on-court situations to estimate your level, pinpoint what usually breaks down in matches, and save the result to track improvement over time.

  • 10 situational questions
  • Instant level estimate
  • Save and compare progress
Start the self-check →

Paddle Finder

Eight questions rank your top three from 24 real paddles — each with the reason it fits your style, level, and budget.

Find my paddle →

Return loop

Four reasons to come back

Picklary is designed as a repeatable learning loop: level check, video review, gear comparison, and update scanning.

Weekly check

Retake the DUPR self-check

Save the result and compare whether your choices changed since the last visit.

Open →Feedback loop

Review one short rally

One ordinary mistake often reveals the next practice topic faster than a highlight.

Open →Gear check

Re-check paddle candidates

When your level or style changes, your gear fit may change too. Compare against benchmark models.

Open →Updates

Check The Brief

Scan the short update habit for rules, gear, and pro-scene changes worth verifying.

Open →

Four ways to use Picklary

Rules & Skills

Learn rules, kitchen play, serve, dinks, third-shot drops, and positioning by level.

Explore →

Gear Lab

Compare popular paddles by brand, style, price band, traits, player line, and review signals.

Explore →

Pro Scene

Browse players, major results, and PPA/MLP rules in one place.

Explore →

Skill Review

Share a video link and get feedback on shot choice, positioning, and repeatable mistakes.

Explore →

Highlight battle

Organize highlight clips with level and skill tags, then use the sample leaderboard and feedback template to build better clip-review habits.

Open Skill Review

Find your next game, partner, coach, or feedback loop

Play Hub turns community needs into clear paths: partners, tournaments, coaches, skill review, Q&A, and level FAQ.

Level FAQ Q&A Board

Latest guides

Skills & Drills

Pickleball for beginners: your complete start-to-3.0 roadmap

A start-here guide for new pickleball players: learn the rules that matter, find your level, master four core shots, pick a sensible first paddle, and follow a simple routine to reach a solid 3.0.

Updated Jun 30, 2026
Tournaments & Leagues

How to use DUPR without overreacting to one number

A balanced guide to using DUPR and community DUPR식 estimates without overreacting to one match, one video, or one rating number.

Updated Jun 28, 2026
Rules & Getting Started

How to play pickleball while traveling for work or vacation

A guide for visiting pickleball players: how to search by city and ZIP code, introduce your level, find open play, and connect respectfully with local groups.

Updated Jun 28, 2026
Skills & Drills

How to choose a pickleball coach for your level

How to choose a pickleball coach based on level fit, skill focus, lesson structure, feedback quality, and local coach/student pools.

Updated Jun 28, 2026 · Level 3.0
Rules & Getting Started

How to find pickleball friends after moving to a new city

A practical guide for new residents and new players looking for pickleball communities, open play, partners, and more competitive groups.

Updated Jun 28, 2026
Skills & Drills

Why backhand resets are hard and how to practice them

A practical guide to backhand resets in pickleball, covering contact point, paddle angle, grip pressure, body control, and drills.

Updated Jun 28, 2026 · Level 3.5

Explore more with Picklary

Explore the blogs and Picklary together

The blogs offer deeper pickleball tips, gear notes, and updates. Picklary gives you practical tools, level guides, and comparisons you can use right away.

Read more pickleball postsCheck my DUPR level

Suggested line: “Curious about your current level? Try the Picklary DUPR self-check.”

Deeper stories on the blogs

Read longer club notes, practice reflections, and gear impressions on the blogs.

Practical tools on Picklary

Use the self-check, level pages, Paddle Finder, and Skill Review to turn what you read into action.

Follow new updates together

Follow new guides, visuals, and result summaries across both the blogs and Picklary.

Editor's picks

Rules & Getting Started

Pickleball rules at a glance: scoring and the serve

A plain-English walkthrough of the serve, the two-bounce rule, and traditional side-out scoring, so your first games run smoothly.

Updated Jun 21, 2026
Paddles & Gear

How to choose your first pickleball paddle

A buying-decision guide that explains the four attributes that actually change how a paddle plays, so you can match one to your game.

Updated Jun 21, 2026
Skills & Drills

Your first 30 days: a practice routine for 2.0–3.0 players

A four-week practice framework for new players, focused on the few skills that raise your level fastest.

Updated Jun 21, 2026 · Level 2.0–3.0

The Brief

A periodic, plain-English digest: what's worth knowing in gear, rules, and the competitive scene — in our own words, each item linking to its primary source. We don't republish other people's articles.

  • Raw carbon-fibre faces keep showing up across new releases. For a 2.0–3.5 player the practical effect is spin-friendly texture and a controlled feel — useful, but not a reason to replace a paddle you still play well with.

    Source: Picklary — paddle face materials

  • If you compete, check the official approved-equipment list before buying, since tournament eligibility is decided there, not by marketing copy.

    Source: USA Pickleball
The Brief

How we write

  • We write for players, not for search engines — every page should help a player choose a level, paddle, player profile, or feedback path.
  • We separate what we have tested from what we compare by published spec — and we say which is which.
  • For anything that changes — rankings, prices, dates, rules — we link to the official source instead of guessing.
  • No fabricated reviews, statistics, or credentials. Ever.
  • Guides are revised as gear and the game evolve, and each shows when it was last updated.

Who runs Picklary

S

Shawn

Pickleball player of 2 years and the person who built Picklary

I'm Shawn. I moved from tennis to pickleball in 2024 and now play doubles about three times a week around Atlanta, at Lifetime Fitness and ACE Pickleball Club. I have no tournament experience, but I enjoy helping newer players, and I buy and test gear myself and write up what I learn. I built Picklary because clear, well-organized information on levels, rules, and gear was hard to find in both Korean and English — so I'm gathering what I study in one place anyone can use. For anything that changes — ratings, prices, rules — Picklary points readers back to official sources.

Questions or corrections?
Picklary

An independent, reader-focused pickleball information site.

By Shawn · iam4na2@gmail.com
